description:
GOAL: Make GC actually run from JIT'd code. Currently `gc()` is invoked
only from Rust tests. JIT'd code allocates indefinitely without
collecting. This ticket bundles three tightly-coupled pieces:

  1. Trampoline-tick safepoint + alloc-edge over-threshold flag.
  2. Frame-chain root walker.
  3. HeapVisitor trait (introduced here because it's the FIRST consumer
     beyond test gc; .19.3 / .19.5 will add their own visitors).

This ticket also SUBSUMES fz-ul4.11.30 (wire ir_liveness or delete) —
.11.30's question is answered while implementing the root walker.

## Decisions captured (user, 2026-05-11)

- Safepoint strategy: HYBRID (option 1d).
  - Trampoline-tick check is primary. Before each frame dispatch in
    `ir_codegen.rs:124-154`, check the current process's heap. If
    over threshold, run GC before reading the next schema_id.
  - Alloc-edge: each `fz_alloc_*` runtime fn sets an AtomicBool flag
    on the heap when occupancy crosses threshold. Trampoline reads +
    clears at the next tick. No GC during the FFI fn itself —
    re-entrance-safe.
- Root-walking: SCHEMA SLOT-KIND with cont_ptr at FIXED OFFSET.
  - `FieldKind` already has `FzValue` and `RawBytes(u32)`. KEEP AS-IS.
  - cont_ptr lives at `frame[16]`. Not a schema slot; walker reads
    structurally.
- AtomicBool not Cell<bool>: libdispatch model means pool size may be
  N>1 in the future; flag becomes shared. Cheap atomic regardless.

## Why bundled, not split

Safepoint with no walker means gc(roots=[]) frees everything (wrong).
Walker with no safepoint has no consumer. HeapVisitor trait without a
consumer is yak-shaving. All three together is the minimum viable
"GC runs from JIT".

## Why we subsume .11.30

ir_liveness::analyze_module is dead code. Two paths:

A. Use ir_liveness as the source of "which slots are FzValue carriers
   across calls" for `build_frame_schema`.
B. Verify the simpler invariant: every IR Var is a tagged FzValue, so
   every Var slot IS FzValue by construction. No per-call analysis
   needed; just confirm and delete ir_liveness.

LEAN TOWARD (B). The IR layer represents all values as tagged 64-bit
FzValues (int = `(n<<3)|TAG_INT`, atoms = `(id<<3)|TAG_ATOM`, floats
are boxed pointers, heap things are pointers). RawBytes slots exist
only in Vec payloads — never in frame schemas. Verify this invariant;
delete ir_liveness.rs and its tests.

Decide at impl time. Either way, .11.30 closes as "subsumed".

## Independence from .11.32

.11.32 (per-Process state) and .11.31 (GC from JIT) are INDEPENDENT.
Whichever lands first:

- If .11.32 first: walker reads roots via `current_process().heap`.
- If .11.31 first: walker reads via existing HEAP TLS. The "current
  frame" comes from the trampoline's `cur` local.

Either way the safepoint sits in the trampoline at the existing
location (`ir_codegen.rs:124-154`).

## Deliverables

### A. HeapVisitor trait (new in src/heap.rs)

```rust
pub trait HeapVisitor {
    fn visit(&mut self, p: *mut HeapHeader, kind: HeapKind) -> WalkDecision;
}

pub enum WalkDecision {
    Recurse,    // walk children
    Skip,       // don't recurse, continue siblings
    Stop,       // halt walk entirely
}

pub fn walk_heap<V: HeapVisitor>(
    root: FzValue,
    reg: &SchemaRegistry,
    visitor: &mut V,
);
```

The existing inline trace logic in Heap::gc becomes a MarkVisitor
implementation of this trait. fz-ul4.19.3 will add CopyVisitor.
fz-ul4.19.5 will add ArenaCheckVisitor.

### B. Frame-chain root walker

```rust
pub fn collect_roots_from_frame_chain(
    cur: *mut HeapHeader,
    schemas: &SchemaRegistry,
) -> Vec<FzValue>;
```

Walks `cur` via frame[16] (cont_ptr). For each frame: read schema,
emit FzValue slots. Visited-set guards cycles (frames don't cycle
structurally but defensive).

### C. Schema invariant assertion (replaces .11.30)

Replace the proposed "schema audit" with: in
`SchemaRegistry::register`, assert that for any schema produced by
codegen (frame schemas, tuple schemas, struct schemas, list cons,
closure, map, bitstring schemas), every field is FzValue EXCEPT for
Vec payloads (VecI64/F64/U8/Bit) and the Float box payload which are
RawBytes. Document and assert.

This collapses the "audit every schema producer" deliverable from the
original ticket into "assert the invariant + delete ir_liveness".

### D. Alloc-edge over-threshold flag (src/heap.rs)

```rust
pub struct Heap {
    // existing fields...
    over_threshold: AtomicBool,
    gc_threshold_bytes: usize,    // default e.g. 1MiB
}

impl Heap {
    fn note_alloc(&self, new_used: usize) {
        if new_used >= self.gc_threshold_bytes {
            self.over_threshold.store(true, Ordering::Relaxed);
        }
    }
    pub fn should_gc(&self) -> bool {
        self.over_threshold.load(Ordering::Relaxed)
    }
    pub fn clear_should_gc_flag(&self) {
        self.over_threshold.store(false, Ordering::Relaxed);
    }
}
```

Wire `note_alloc` at the bottom of each `Heap::alloc_*`.

### E. Trampoline integration (src/ir_codegen.rs:124-154)

```rust
loop {
    if cur.is_null() { break; }
    let heap = /* current process's heap */;
    if heap.should_gc() {
        let roots = collect_roots_from_frame_chain(cur, &heap.schemas);
        heap.gc(&roots);
        heap.clear_should_gc_flag();
    }
    // existing dispatch...
}
```

If .11.32 has landed: `current_process().heap`. If not: `HEAP.with(...)`.

## Tests (TDD order)

1. HeapVisitor unit test: a MarkVisitor over a small list walks every
   cons cell. CopyVisitor (mock) records each visit.
2. Root walker on a 3-frame chain with known schemas returns the
   expected FzValue set.
3. Walker terminates on null cont_ptr.
4. Alloc that crosses threshold sets the AtomicBool flag.
5. Manual trampoline run that allocates past threshold; assert
   gc_run_count >= 1 via instrumentation.
6. Hot-loop pattern: 1000 list-cons-cell allocations with only the
   final-list reference live in an entry frame. After full run,
   live_count is small; gc_run_count >= 1.
7. Schema invariant: SchemaRegistry::register rejects a schema with a
   mis-tagged slot (or asserts at debug-build time).
8. SAMPLE-based tests still pass.

## Risks

- Schema invariant might surface schema producers we missed.
  Mitigation: per-HeapKind test runs schema registration through the
  invariant check.
- HeapVisitor as a trait may be over-engineered if v1 only has one
  visitor. Mitigation: .19.3 and .19.5 already need it; introducing
  the trait now (with one consumer) is correct.
- ir_liveness deletion may surface that we DID need it for something.
  Mitigation: grep for `ir_liveness` uses BEFORE deletion; verify the
  module's `analyze_module` is genuinely dead.

## Out of scope

- Generational nursery (follow-up).
- Concurrent / incremental GC (follow-up).
- Write barriers (no mutation in fz).
- Per-process scheduler integration: that's .19.4's verification work.

## References

- src/heap.rs:73-96 (Heap struct)
- src/heap.rs gc() + trace walker (refactored under HeapVisitor)
- src/heap.rs:25-28 (FieldKind — staying as-is)
- src/ir_codegen.rs:124-154 (trampoline)
- src/ir_codegen.rs:129 (cont_ptr at frame[16])
- src/ir_liveness.rs (deleted at end of this ticket if option B)
- fz-ul4.11.32 (per-Process state — independent; either ordering works)
- fz-ul4.11.30 (SUBSUMED — close on commit of .11.31)

## Acceptance

- HeapVisitor trait introduced; MarkVisitor implements it; existing
  trace logic relocated to it.
- Frame-chain root walker working.
- Trampoline safepoint check present.
- gc_run_count increases under hot allocation.
- Schema invariant assertion in place.
- ir_liveness.rs deleted (or kept with rationale, decided at impl).
- No regressions.
- fz-ul4.11.30 closed as "subsumed".
- One commit referencing fz-ul4.11.31.
title: Real GC: trampoline-tick safepoints + frame-chain root walker
